35232142: Flooding in Dumfries and Galloway and the Scottish Borders has caused significant damage, with many areas still assessing the full cost and residents calling for improved flood prevention measures.
40143035: A fire deliberately started under one of two buses parked at a Northern Ireland hotel, destroying personal belongings and forcing tour groups to reschedule their itinerary.
35951548: Mercedes secured a dominant qualifying performance, with Lewis Hamilton taking pole position by 0.077 seconds over Nico Rosberg, ahead of Ferrari's Sebastian Vettel.
36266422: John Edward Bates, a 67-year-old former police officer, faces 22 charges, including indecency with a child, related to alleged abuse as a scout leader between 1972 and 1989.
38826984: A young police officer, receiving psychiatric treatment, threatened to shoot himself and others at Cerahpasa hospital in Istanbul, prompting an evacuation and police negotiation.
34540833: Glasgow Warriors secured a narrow victory over the Dragons, capitalizing on their bench experience and superior strength in depth to overcome a messy match.
20836172: A 31-year-old Ecuadorian woman, Veronica Vanessa Chango-Alverez, was killed in a car crash in London, with police seeking a 27-year-old man linked to the Audi involved.
35932467: Two Belgian cyclists, Demoitie and Myngheer, died in separate incidents during professional cycling events, prompting calls for improved safety measures and a reevaluation of racing protocols.
40758845: Ilkay Gundogan, recovering from a knee injury, expressed optimism about Manchester City's season, citing the team's experience and the threat posed by Tottenham in their upcoming match.
30358490: A man in his 20s was seriously injured in a crash on the A127 in Essex, airlifted to the Royal London Hospital for further treatment.
34615665: A Category 5 tropical storm, the highest rating, has hit mainland Mexico for the first time since 1959, causing significant damage and disruption.
35890902: Researchers from Bastille Networks discovered vulnerabilities in wireless mice that allowed attackers to spoof signals and take control of computers, affecting products from major manufacturers.
37922330: Murray and Burrell, a 95-year-old Galashiels-based building firm, has entered administration, resulting in 38 redundancies due to adverse trading conditions.
30844962: The European Commission has launched an investigation into Luxembourg's tax arrangement with Amazon, citing concerns over potential state aid and tax avoidance.
36217333: The Balmoral Show, a 148-year-old agricultural event, returns to Balmoral Park in Northern Ireland, featuring local produce, livestock, and family-friendly activities for over 90,000 visitors.
30816523: Nigel Mosley is suing Google for breaching the Data Protection Act and misusing private information, seeking removal of explicit photos from its search results.
38900884: Dragons forwards coach Ceri Jones considers the injury to Eddie Jackson a significant blow, but also a chance for other players to step up and showcase their abilities.
13890581: The UK government has secured a three-year funding package for the Cornish Language Partnership, ensuring the survival of the critically endangered language.
40194700: AS Monaco's manager, Leonardo Jardim, described the club's last three years as "exceptional," having won the French championship and reached the Champions League semi-finals.
37903647: Nicaraguan President Daniel Ortega won a landslide election with 72.5% of the vote, but the State Department and opposition coalition questioned the election's legitimacy and fairness.
13291223: South Africa's government has announced a task team to address "corrective rape" after 170,000 activists worldwide demanded action to protect lesbian victims.
36052570: Global stock markets declined, with energy and tech sectors hit particularly hard, as investors digested a gloomy economic report and oil production freeze talks.
34944735: US biologists found that the vibrant blue color in tarantulas evolved independently at least eight times, suggesting it serves an important visual signaling function.
32159602: Andreas Lubitz, a 27-year-old Germanwings co-pilot, intentionally crashed Flight 9525, killing 150 people, after researching suicide methods and cockpit security in the days leading up to the crash.
34988915: DJ Mr Fox, a well-known radio presenter, denies eight counts of indecent assault and two counts of sexual assault, claiming his past behavior was consensual "horseplay".
37889203: Tottenham midfielder Dele Alli is expected to miss several upcoming England matches due to a knee injury sustained in a training incident with a teammate.
33609927: The Jehovah's Witnesses' policy of dealing with child abuse in-house has been criticized for prioritizing internal justice over reporting to authorities, allowing perpetrators to evade accountability.
33578778: Greece's bailout negotiations with the eurozone have begun, with a €86bn package contingent on austerity measures, despite warnings of impending program failure.
36888270: The International Paralympic Committee (IPC) is considering a ban on Russia's National Paralympic Committee due to allegations of a widespread state-sponsored doping program.
36846007: The Exiles rugby team has signed Ben, a 24-year-old former England under-20 international, to bolster their squad ahead of a competitive campaign.
31723471: Manor F1, having secured investment from Stephen Fitzpatrick and appointed Justin King as interim chairman, aims to be competitive in the upcoming season.
15921828: The Welsh government's £38.9m stimulus package, agreed as part of its budget deal with the Liberal Democrats, aims to generate immediate economic benefits through various projects and initiatives.
39109408: Former UK Prime Minister Sir John Major warned that Brexit will be a "historic mistake" and urged the government to adopt a more diplomatic approach in negotiations with the EU.
20936833: The UK government's Welfare Benefits Uprating Bill, which caps benefit rises at 1% for three years, is set to pass in the House of Commons despite opposition from Labour and some Lib Dem MPs.
36832879: A collapsed sewer under a railway track in Forest Hill has caused significant disruptions to Southern, Thameslink, and London Overground services until Wednesday.
14958201: Manchester City secured a 2-0 win over Everton, with Mario Balotelli and James Milner scoring in the second half to move the team to the top of the Premier League.
34352262: Scottish First Minister Nicola Sturgeon faced criticism for claiming her government had met a target to reduce six-week delays in discharging patients, despite Labour saying it had not been met for over three years.
39805395: The European Court of Justice ruled that the UK's overflow pipes in a Welsh conservation area broke clean water laws, despite the UK's planned improvements.
34303109: Pope Francis, the first Latin American pontiff, arrived in Cuba, calling for reconciliation and further support for the island's Catholics, before heading to the US.
39939090: Rangi Chase, a 31-year-old former England playmaker, has joined Widnes on loan from Castleford, seeking regular playing time and a fresh start.
29347771: Roddy Smith, Cricket Scotland's chief executive, is stepping down after a decade, leaving the organization in good health with a growing game and a strong future outlook.
36783415: Sunderland manager Sam Allardyce is the frontrunner for the England job, despite Premier League club Sunderland wanting him to stay as their manager.
37935687: Wales secured a 24-20 victory over Argentina, with Leigh Halfpenny's 14 points proving decisive in a match that saw the Pumas refuse to give up.
40260829: Two monuments, the Bulford Kiwi and the Terrain Model of Messines, have been protected as scheduled monuments to honor New Zealand's bravery in World War I.
36478199: Two inmates at HMP Wayland, a Category C men's prison in Norfolk, were transferred to a higher security jail after attempting to escape and steal keys.
34541803: Former Norwich and Blackburn player Chris Sutton believes Aston Villa manager Tim Sherwood deserves more time to turn the team's fortunes around despite a poor start.
35360841: Welsh Transport Minister Edwina Hart aims to reform the system, addressing concerns over rejected claims and fraud, through new guidelines and a consultation process.
35550407: Life expectancy in England has generally increased for those aged 65, but with variations across regions and a concerning trend of poor health among elderly people.
37561590: Operation Equinox, a combined police inquiry, has made significant progress in investigating historical abuse claims in Nottinghamshire, with 290 reported crimes and several convictions already secured.
39328843: The Women's FA Cup quarterfinals feature a mix of top-flight teams and newcomers, with Chelsea, Arsenal, and Manchester City among the favorites to advance.
35389665: Cheltenham Town secured their seventh win in eight games in the National League, with Holman and Wright both scoring doubles in a dominant performance.
33080187: Jean Charles de Menezes, a Brazilian electrician, was fatally shot by police at Stockwell Tube station in London on July 22, 2005, in a mistaken identity incident.
38636995: Glasgow City Council plans to increase the council tax by 3% to address a £67m budget gap, while protecting vulnerable households and frontline services.
18536236: The Scottish government's approval of the Viking Energy wind farm, set to power over 175,000 homes, has sparked opposition from local group Sustainable Shetland.
36289151: Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye was charged with treason after being arrested and flown to a remote court, amidst allegations of election rigging and authoritarian rule.
23017045: The British and Irish Lions secured a 23-21 win over Australia in Brisbane, with George North scoring his first Lions Test try and Wales teammate Alex Cuthbert adding another.
36418082: Germany's national team has announced its 27-man squad for the European Championship, with several notable players, including Reus and Schweinsteiger, missing due to injury concerns.
34396551: Fifa president Sepp Blatter and former Uefa president Michel Platini are accused of making a "disloyal payment" of $2m, with both men denying wrongdoing.
39720944: John Higgins advanced to the World Snooker Championship semi-finals with a 13-6 win over Kyren Wilson, setting up a potential showdown with fellow Scot Stephen Maguire.
35884842: Pte Paul Wilkinson testified at the inquest into Pte Cheryl James's death, denying any involvement and claiming he was simply upset and humiliated after being caught with her by her boyfriend.
35403375: Doug Richard, a 57-year-old US citizen, is on trial for allegedly paying a 13-year-old girl and her friend for sex, claiming he believed the girl was over 16.
26341324: Vandalism in Ealing damaged 11 cables, disrupting internet services for some BT, Plusnet, and Talk Talk customers, although most have since been reconnected.
19212345: Caster Semenya, a 21-year-old South African, is a top contender for the Olympic 800m title, with her coach, Maria Mutola, guiding her preparations in London.
30548367: A SXSW festival-goer's death and multiple injuries led to lawsuits accusing organizers of serious safety lapses, citing inadequate traffic safeguards during the event.
37919402: British sailing duo Vicky Mills and Saskia Clark won Olympic gold in the women's 470 event at Rio 2016, improving on their London silver medal.
39995133: World-ranked 10th fencer Alexander Massialas Kruse won the Shanghai foil title, defeating Italy's Alessio Foconi 15-8 and rising to world number five.
40249088: Ineos plans to expand its ethylene and propylene production in Europe, increasing capacity by 50% and creating 300 new jobs at two locations.
40254388: Uber's board has unanimously adopted a report's recommendations, including increased control on spending and leadership changes, following a review of the company's culture and practices.
31995230: English Heritage has agreed to allow the replacement of Charminster bridge with a new one featuring larger arches, while preserving its historic character and original materials.
38632129: The top six Premier League teams are shaping up, with Chelsea firmly in control, Tottenham and Liverpool close behind, and Arsenal and Manchester City struggling to keep pace.
35720795: Celtic midfielder McGregor was fined £500 and banned from driving for 12 months after being caught driving with a blood alcohol level more than three times the legal limit.
23906759: Dan Evans' impressive US Open campaign came to an end with a 7-6 (8-6) 6-1 4-6 7-5 loss to Tommy Robredo in a thrilling third-round match.
19410108: The Indian Supreme Court's decision to uphold the death penalty for Mohammad Ajmal Amir Qasab has reignited a long-standing debate over the effectiveness and morality of capital punishment in the country.
30745137: The Hoegh Osaka, a 51,000-tonne vehicle transporter, is being pumped out of water after being deliberately run aground off the UK coast, with salvage efforts ongoing.
26553115: Researchers have discovered a diamond containing a water-bearing mineral, ringwoodite, which suggests that the Earth's interior may store several times the amount of water in the oceans.
37872311: UKIP's leadership contest continues with Paul Nuttall, Suzanne Evans, and John Rees-Evans remaining in the running, following Raheem Kassam's withdrawal and Nigel Farage's return as interim leader.
35553131: Aberdeen-born artist receives the RSGS's Livingstone Medal, a prestigious award first given in 1901, for raising awareness about the global HIV/AIDS pandemic.
39038936: News organisations are suing the FBI to reveal the identity of a security firm that helped unlock the iPhone of the San Bernardino killer in 2015.
38735486: Vernon Cotter, Scotland's outgoing coach, reflects on his two-and-a-half-year tenure, citing progress, leadership, and a renewed Scottish rugby identity as his lasting legacies.
17087845: At least 358 people, mostly inmates, died in a Honduran prison fire, with officials investigating the causes and President Lobo promising prison safety reforms.
37443011: The Willow Tea Rooms Trust is refurbishing the historic Mackintosh-designed building on Sauchiehall Street, restoring it to its original grandeur while honoring its past.
36855749: Michelle Obama showcased her singing and rapping skills on The Late, Late Show with James Corden, promoting her Let Girls Learn initiative with Missy Elliott.
35613141: England's batting display faltered, but their bowlers nearly pulled off a stunning comeback in a 15-run loss to South Africa in the first T20 international.
39088847: The UK government has announced changes to Personal Independence Payments (PIPs) that could affect over 160,000 disabled people, despite criticism from Labour and disability rights campaigners.
33197277: Olly Murs and Caroline Flack will co-host The Xtra Factor, a spin-off show to The X Factor, alongside new judges Nick Grimshaw and Rita Ora.
33815489: William Hill's pre-tax profits fell 35% to £78.7m in the first six months of the year due to increased taxation and one-off costs.
35862754: Widnes Vikings' strong season, led by their defensive improvement and cohesive team atmosphere, has them atop the Super League table with seven games remaining.
36080615: The Great Barrier Reef is experiencing its worst coral bleaching event on record, with only 7% of the reef showing no signs of bleaching.
22822742: Manchester United has signed 20-year-old Uruguayan defender Guillermo Varela, who made one appearance for Penarol and nine caps for Uruguay's Under-20 side.
39747536: Granada CF suffered a 2-1 loss to Real Sociedad, with Carlos Vela and Juanmi scoring for the hosts, but ultimately sealing their relegation from the top flight.
34218396: Twelve men, accused of planning and executing the 2006 Mumbai train bombings that killed 189 people, are set to face sentencing after an eight-year trial.
39150388: A new study by Cancer Research UK found that over a third of Scots consume confectionery daily, with obesity being the single biggest cause of preventable cancer after smoking.
28765782: Wisconsin Governor Scott Walker declared "Purple Hearts for Healing" Day in honor of a 12-year-old girl stabbed 19 times by two classmates who claimed to be acting in dedication to the fictional character Slenderman.
35828022: Greek coach Skafidas admitted to nine rule violations, including abuse of trust, related to his conduct with sprinter Wilson, who received a reduced ban for cooperation.
27717735: The Edinburgh trams project, plagued by cost overruns, delays, and disputes, has finally begun operation after six years, prompting a judge-led inquiry into its conduct.
37977826: Italian MotoGP rider Valentino Rossi apologized for colliding with a fan at the Valencia Grand Prix, but the fan, Ana Cabanillas Vazquez, disputes it was an accident.
31768588: Edinburgh Rugby's Kennedy is expected to be sidelined for up to six months due to an ongoing shoulder complaint, requiring surgery.
37183351: North Cumbria University Hospitals NHS Trust is considering removing consultant-led services from Whitehaven's West Cumberland Hospital due to concerns over recruitment and infrastructure.
39622090: Former Rangers player Morten Lovenkrands believes his old team can cause an upset against Celtic in the Scottish Cup semi-final, citing the unpredictability of Old Firm games.
39272756: The PSNI will close off-licences in the Holylands area of Belfast from 16:00-21:00 GMT on St Patrick's Day to help maintain public safety.
32138822: South Wales Police are investigating a robbery at Lloyds Bank in Canton, seeking a suspect captured on CCTV with a distinctive appearance.
31070114: The UK's 77th Brigade, a new military unit, will focus on "smarter" warfare, using social media, psychological operations, and civilians with specialized skills to influence local populations.
39652762: Garry Allen, 33, has been charged with causing death by dangerous driving after a fatal crash on the A52 in Bottesford on May 25, 2016.
33258866: Sarah Sands, 32, denies murdering her 77-year-old neighbor Michael Pleasted, who was awaiting trial for allegedly abusing three boys, after a confrontation in his flat.
36962388: A report by Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch alleges that Australia's off-shore detention centre on Nauru allows abuse to deter asylum seekers, citing claims of rape and inadequate care.
32282272: Wales' main election issue, the NHS, is at the forefront of party debates, with Labour and Conservatives clashing over funding, privatization, and healthcare services.
36093072: A 13ft by 9ft wooden beach hut in Abersoch sold for a record-breaking price, exceeding the previous record by nearly £40,000.
38879972: The 2017 Fifa video game scandal involved third-party websites that exploited game vulnerabilities, not endorsed by Fifa or EA Sports.
32106473: A residents' parking zone in Clifton Village, Bristol, has sparked controversy over the installation of signs on Grade II* listed railings in a conservation area.
40415815: A four-day forest fire in Portugal resulted in 64 deaths and 254 injuries, with officials citing failures in the Siresp emergency services network as a contributing factor.
34581918: Fifa's presidential election is underway, with several candidates vying for the top spot, amidst corruption allegations and ongoing reforms aimed at increasing transparency.
33004157: A new automated system has been introduced at the Blackhall Place-Benburb Street junction on Dublin's Luas red line to deter motorists from breaking red lights.
30334943: An 18-year-old man, dressed as Batman, was charged with entering a restricted area at Sunderland's Stadium of Light during a Manchester City match.
33280503: The UK monarch, accompanied by Prince Philip, visited the Bergen-Belsen concentration camp, paying respects to the victims and hearing stories from survivors and liberators.
25726782: Dumfries and Galloway Council is investing almost £2.5m in a harbour redevelopment project, aiming to transform the area into a marina complex and boost town regeneration.
35479854: Midlands club Villa suffered a 2-0 loss to West Ham, with Villa's manager Garde expressing frustration over a rejected penalty appeal and Jordan Ayew's early red card.
32716226: Dane County District Attorney Ishmael Ozanne decided not to charge police officer Matt Kenny in the fatal shooting of 19-year-old Tony Robinson Jr, citing a lawful use of deadly force.
37047554: The UK government has named and shamed 198 employers who failed to pay their workers the minimum wage, with a total of £3.5m in arrears owed to 688 employees.
36565290: The M4 motorway in Wales will be closed between junctions 25A and 26 for up to five nights a week until February 2018 for tunnel maintenance.
27758640: Thousands of Lyssa Zampa tropical moths, native to South East Asia, have been spotted in Malaysia and Singapore, sparking debate and fascination among locals and experts alike.
28897118: The Madagascar pochard, a critically endangered duck species, is facing extinction due to starvation in its last remaining wetland habitat in north-east Madagascar.
38452661: Ireland's T20 squad, bolstered by the return of experienced internationals, will participate in a week-long round-robin competition in the United Arab Emirates.
38033492: Hywel Dda University Health Board plans to reduce the hours of its paediatric ambulatory care unit at Withybush Hospital due to a shortage of consultant paediatricians.
35949555: The match saw Stirling's Lewis McLear strike the post early, while the Binos' Sean Dickson forced a spectacular save from City goalkeeper Mark Hurst.
15263826: Kazakhstan, a vast and ethnically diverse country, has transitioned from Soviet rule to independence, with a complex history marked by rapid economic growth and authoritarian leadership.
37313866: British athletes continued to compete in various events at the Games, with several notable performances, including Rob Davies' strong table tennis start and Rachel Morris' para-rowing campaign.
13911157: Wimbledon has signed centre-half Mitchel-King, a former Histon player, after an extensive medical, and also exercised contract options on forward Luke Moore and defender Fraser Franks.

34096170: Scottish midfielder Shaun Maloney has signed a two-year deal with Hull City, citing personal and professional reasons for leaving Chicago and returning to British football.
37891952: British short track speed skater Elise Christie won a gold medal in Calgary, finishing ahead of Canadian Jamie Macdonald and Netherlands' Yara van Kerkhof.
40186158: Douglas Innes, the yacht manager of Cheeki Rafiki, is on trial for manslaughter by gross negligence after the yacht capsized in the North Atlantic in 2014.
37049719: Swansea City's new signing, Borja, expressed enthusiasm for his move, citing the club's confidence in its players and his goal to make a good impression.
34610504: Sporting Khalsa, a semi-professional Asian football club from the West Midlands, is set to make history by hosting FC United of Manchester in the FA Cup fourth qualifying round.
37595429: A 45-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of a racially aggravated offense and possessing an offensive weapon during a Rosh Hashanah celebration in Greater Manchester.
29772670: Johann Lamont, the leader of the Scottish Labour Party, has resigned, citing a need for a "real discussion" about the party's future in Scotland.

34527433: Bank of America reported a 5% rise in consumer banking profits to $1.8bn, driven by deposit growth and stable trading results, while Wells Fargo saw its first profit increase in three quarters.
31111734: A documentary, "Mr Alzheimers and Me," follows three young people, including Josh, as they share their personal stories about living with grandparents who have dementia.
40303872: Campbell Baird rescued a cat named Francee who had become stuck in a wall cavity at his new home, reuniting her with her relieved owner.
33653553: Shamsul Arefin was found guilty of modern-day slavery and human trafficking crimes, exploiting four Bangladeshi men who were forced to work in poor conditions at his Scottish hotel.
34404510: The London Paramount resort, a massive 900-acre development with 5,000 hotel rooms and a water park, has delayed its submission for approval due to ongoing research.
35568692: A fragile ceasefire agreement in Syria faces significant challenges as world powers, including Russia and the US, disagree on the conflict's causes and solutions.
39151664: Leyton Orient, a relegation-threatened English football club, faces a winding-up petition from tax authorities, prompting fans to launch a crowdfunding campaign and seek an administrator.

40319923: A 38-year-old man, Shaun Murphy, has died in hospital after being found injured in Wythenshawe, with a 23-year-old suspect in custody for questioning.

32283530: A high-speed crash involving two supercars, a Lamborghini and a Ferrari, occurred in a tunnel near Shanghai's national stadium, injuring one person and causing significant damage.

34281974: East Sussex Healthcare Trust, rated "inadequate" by the Care Quality Commission, is likely to be placed into special measures due to ongoing leadership and improvement issues.

35584972: Students at Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) protest the arrest of their student union leader Kanhaiya Kumar, who faces sedition charges over an event commemorating a convicted Parliament attacker.
39277709: New Zealand's 35-year-old wicket-keeper batsman Luke Ronchi joins the Pakistan Super League, replacing Sharjeel Khan, who's provisionally suspended in an anti-corruption investigation.
36123657: Police investigated an incident on the Glen Road, but after searches, they found nothing and the road has since reopened, with the object's nature still unknown.

37349129: The Singapore Grand Prix stands out as one of the most desirable and challenging races on the F1 calendar, offering a unique blend of atmosphere and competition.
37160199: A quadriplegic man claims a "do not resuscitate" order was placed on his medical file without his consent, sparking concerns about patient autonomy and medical decision-making.

36185624: Oyster farming in Porlock Bay has become a successful venture, securing a top cleanliness rating and aiming to sell 500,000 oysters within four to five years.

30725298: Glasgow's City Chambers flew the French flag at half-mast in solidarity with the victims of the Charlie Hebdo attack and to condemn the brutal act of terrorism.

37383994: In the Hackney by-election, Mr Glanville won 69% of the votes, maintaining Labour's control of the borough with a significant majority.

35499522: New South Wales Premier Mike Baird's defense of the "lockout laws" has sparked widespread criticism and outrage, with many arguing they've killed Sydney's nightlife.
30157766: England's one-day international form has been inconsistent, but Finn believes the team's underdog status at the World Cup could be an advantage.

33698679: The US central bank kept interest rates at a record low, citing a moderately expanding economy, but hinted at a potential rate hike in September.
40530172: Share your Scotland photos by sending them to scotlandpictures@bbc.co.uk or posting on Instagram with #bbcscotlandpics, and be part of a captivating visual showcase.
40652941: A 22-year-old barber from Newtownabbey, County Antrim, has gone missing, and police are seeking information about his disappearance, last seen on July 13.

37680058: A UK tribunal ruled that some data collection by spy agencies did not comply with human rights laws, but proper supervision was later put in place.
37291821: A rocket attack on Turkish tanks in Syria resulted in the deaths of at least three soldiers and wounded five others, with two Syrian rebels also killed.
41002743: The University of Texas at Austin removed statues of Confederate figures, citing their association with modern white supremacy and neo-Nazism.

35682367: Premier Inn is investing in Edinburgh with three new hotels, including a hi-tech "hub by Premier Inn," bringing 150 jobs and 10 apprenticeships to the city.

34965328: Alex Kersey-Brown, a former Wales schoolboy rugby union international, has passed away after a long battle with cancer, leaving behind a legacy in the rugby community.
